description_long: This object is a traditional stone water well with a circular, gray stone base constructed from large, block-like segments. The well is topped with a wooden canopy supported by two vertical wooden posts, featuring a steeply pitched, reddish-brown shingled roof with a distinct overhang. The roof is constructed from wooden beams and has a pronounced triangular gable on two sides, giving it a classic, rustic appearance. The interior of the well is open and appears hollow, with the stone wall forming a protective barrier around the opening. There is no visible pulley, bucket, or water-drawing mechanism attached to the structure. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or embellishments.
description: A circular stone water well with a pitched, reddish-brown wooden roof and open interior, supported by sturdy wooden posts. The structure is simple and functional, lacking any visible water-drawing mechanism or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triangular gable of the wooden roof, central supporting post, and the open stone well beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full span of the pitched roof, both supporting posts, and the circular stone base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the gable roof and central post visible, and the open well interior.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's overhang, both posts, and the stone well's rounded form.
attribute_well_cover_design: The well is covered by a pitched, reddish-brown wooden roof supported by two posts; there is no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall surrounds the well opening, approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier.
